channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Poverty in New York City Inched Higher in 2024 for Third Year in a Row
item.description: The cost of basics like housing and food rose, while incomes and other resources, like public benefits, did not keep up.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth A. Harris

media:content.media:credit: Anna Watts for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An encampment in Flushing Meadows Corona Park in Queens.

item.title: Qué tan saludable es la col o repollo
item.description: No te dejes engañar por su apariencia sencilla, esta verdura tiene una gran concentración de nutrientes.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Pattillo

media:content.media:credit: Suzanne Saroff

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Hints for March 16, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: The Epstein Files: A Timeline
item.description: What began as a sex-trafficking investigation in Florida has gone on to spawn conspiracy theories and tarnish the legacies of influential people.
item.dc:creator: Michael LaForgia

media:content.media:credit: Us Department of Justice
media:content.media:description: Jeffrey Epstein was accused of sexually abusing dozens of underage victims.

item.title: One of Epstein’s Levers of Power: Access to Elite Private Schools
item.description: Jeffrey Epstein used his money and influence in the world of elite private schools to assist friends and acquaintances.
item.dc:creator: Jan Ransom
item.category: Ethical Culture Fieldston School (New York, NY)

media:content.media:credit: Robert Caplin for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Trinity School in New York.

item.title: Hydropower Line From Quebec to Queens Could Power a Million N.Y.C. Homes
item.description: A 339-mile buried transmission line is on schedule to bring clean electricity to New York City this spring.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard

media:content.media:credit: Clark Hodgin for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Part of the Champlain Hudson Power Express converter station in Astoria, Queens.

item.title: Why Did Trump Officials Award $2 Million to a Small Art School in Queens?
item.description: The National Endowment for the Humanities seldom gave seven-figure grants. Now big awards flow to handpicked projects, including an institution with three full-time employees.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Small, Jennifer Schuessler and Marcus Maddox

media:content.media:description: Jacob Collins, the founder and artistic director of Grand Central Atelier, has had a lifetime mission to inspire a return to a classical style of art.

item.title: N.Y.C. to Get Green Energy From Canadian Hydropower
item.description: Our climate reporter Hilary Howard explores the Champlain Hudson Power Express, a new transmission line that will deliver hydro power from Quebec to New York City. It’s planned to provide up to 20% of the city’s energy needs.
item.dc:creator: Hilary Howard, Edward Vega, June Kim, Luke Piotrowski and Gabriel Blanco

item.title: Behind Mamdani’s Smile, a Politician Who Can Be a Ruthless Operator
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani, while charming in public, has thrown sharp elbows in private as he pushes his political brand and agenda among fellow Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Fandos and Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani has wielded his power in personal and often surprising ways behind the scenes, allies say.

item.title: Iran War Live Updates: Trump Pressures Countries to Open Vital Shipping Route
item.description: As fighting in the Middle East entered its third week, President Trump warned that failing to help secure the Strait of Hormuz would be “very bad for the future of NATO.”
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Palabra del día en inglés: ‘cultivate’
item.description: Esta palabra ha aparecido en 359 artículos en NYTimes.com en el último año. ¿Puedes usarla en una frase?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: Word of the Day: cultivate
item.description: This word has appeared in 356 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: ‘One Battle After Another Was Totally Meh’: 3 Opinion Writers Size Up the Oscars
item.description: What did the academy get right? Wrong? What was just weird? Three culture fans discuss Hollywood’s biggest night.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Cottle, Robin Givhan, Naveen Kumar and Stephen Stromberg
item.category: Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Matthew Johnson for The New York Times

item.title: La casa inteligente nunca ha funcionado del todo, pero ahora tiene IA
item.description: Amazon y Google creen que los asistentes de inteligencia artificial como Alexa+ y Gemini acelerarán el proceso de instalación de una casa inteligente, pero quedan muchos problemas por resolver.
item.dc:creator: Brian X. Chen

media:content.media:credit: Sisi Yu

item.title: Michael B. Jordan gana el Oscar al mejor actor por ‘Pecadores’
item.description: Esta fue la primera nominación del actor a un premio de la Academia.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

item.title: ‘Imperfect Women,’ Plus 7 Things to Watch on TV This Week
item.description: A new show starring Elisabeth Moss and Kerry Washington premieres. And Taylor Frankie Paul leads the new season of “The Bachelorette.”
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Courtesy of Apple TV.
media:content.media:description: Elisabeth Moss, left, and Kerry Washington in “Imperfect Women.”

item.title: A Weakened Iran Won’t Transform the Middle East
item.description: A defeated Iran will not produce the transformation of the Middle East sought by many.
item.dc:creator: Daniel C. Kurtzer and Aaron David Miller
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by The New York Times

item.title: The War Is Making It Harder to Keep the Lights On, 2,000 Miles Away
item.description: Bangladesh is taking steps to conserve electricity, which its factories need to keep stitching together the world’s clothing.
item.dc:creator: Saif Hasnat and Alex Travelli
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Fabeha Monir for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “Bangladeshis are very good at doing one thing, like ready-made garments,” said Mohiuddin Rubel, who runs several garment factories. “We are not diversified.”

item.title: Quote of the Day: Belonging To America, But True To Herself
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Monday, March 16, 2026.

item.title: Unforgettable Oscars Looks 2026: Bumblebee Brooches, Jewel-Tone Gowns and More
item.description: Jewel-tone gowns, bumblebee brooches, overalls and more.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

media:content.media:credit: Daniel Cole/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Jessie Buckley packed a one-two punch in two-tone Chanel.

item.title: Oscar 2026: nuestras fotos favoritas de la alfombra roja
item.description: Entradas majestuosas, moda llamativa y uno que otro momento espontáneo, en una noche marcada por el oro.
item.dc:creator: Sinna Nasseri

media:content.media:credit: Sinna Nasseri for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Teyana Taylor, nominated for best supporting actress in “One Battle After Another.”

item.title: Javier Bardem and Other Stars Address Wars and Free Speech at Oscars
item.description: Conan O’Brien, the ceremony’s host, noted in his opening monologue that viewers were “all too aware that these are very chaotic, frightening times.”
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jimmy Kimmel delivered one of the sharpest political jokes of Oscars night while introducing the documentary awards.

item.title: ‘One Battle After Another’ Wins Best Picture at 2026 Oscars After Awards Sweep
item.description: The epic about radicals and reactionaries also took best director for Paul Thomas Anderson, his first Academy Awards after 11 losses.
item.dc:creator: Kyle Buchanan

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Paul Thomas Anderson and the producer Sara Murphy accepted the Oscar for best picture as cast and crew members watched.

item.title: Oscar 2026: fotos de la alfombra roja
item.description: Este año reinaron los atuendos en blanco y negro. Esto es lo que llevaron los nominados, presentadores y artistas para su entrada triunfal del año.
item.dc:creator: The Styles Desk

item.title: Kennedy Center Board to Vote Monday on Trump’s Proposed Closure
item.description: The planned vote to close the center for renovations was listed on an agenda circulated to the center’s board of trustees on Sunday, less than a day before the meeting.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.category: Kennedy, John F, Center for the Performing Arts

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump abruptly announced in February that he would close the Kennedy Center for two years for renovations.

item.title: Jessie Buckley Wins First Oscar for Best Actress in ‘Hamnet’
item.description: The actress closes out a season of wins in this category with an Academy Award.
item.dc:creator: Esther Zuckerman

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jessie Buckley accepting her best actress Oscar for “Hamnet.”

item.title: Michael B. Jordan Wins the Best Actor Oscar for ‘Sinners’
item.description: It was the actor’s first nomination for an Academy Award.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Michael B. Jordan, who played twins in “Sinners,” won the Oscar for best actor on Sunday night.

item.title: Paul Thomas Anderson Wins Best Director Oscar for ‘One Battle After Another’
item.description: It was his second win, after taking home the award for best adapted screenplay on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Esther Zuckerman

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Paul Thomas Anderson on the Oscars stage.

item.title: La desesperación en Cuba provoca actos de desafío poco habituales
item.description: Una protesta en la ciudad de Morón, en el centro de Cuba, terminó con un intento de incendio en la sede local del Partido Comunista.
item.dc:creator: Emiliano Rodríguez Mega

media:content.media:credit: Social media, via Reuters

item.title: Sean Penn, mejor actor de reparto en los Oscar, falta a la ceremonia
item.description: El actor está vinculado con el presidente del país, Volodímir Zelenski, y ha estado faltando a las ceremonias de entrega de premios.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein

media:content.media:credit: Arnaud Finistre/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Sean Penn en un festival en Francia, en octubre

item.title: Chase Infiniti Among Several Stars to Embrace Subtle Makeup at 2026 Oscars
item.description: On the Oscars red carpet, many celebrities opted for subtle pops of color over dramatic, high-glam makeup.
item.dc:creator: Alisha Haridasani Gupta

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Chase Infiniti’s subtle makeup matched her custom Louis Vuitton lilac gown.

item.title: Autumn Durald Arkapaw Makes History as First Woman to Win Best Cinematography
item.description: The “Sinners” cinematographer was also the first woman of color to win that category. “I really want all the women in the room to stand up,” she said in her acceptance speech.
item.dc:creator: Melena Ryzik

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Autumn Durald Arkapaw accepting her Oscar for best cinematography. She also thanked predecessors like Ellen Kuras and Rachel Morrison.

item.title: A Surprising Oscars Tie Is the Seventh in Academy Awards History
item.description: In 1969, Katharine Hepburn and Barbra Streisand each received an Oscar in the best actress category.
item.dc:creator: Livia Albeck-Ripka

media:content.media:credit: Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Barbra Streisand stood beside the director Anthony Harvey on the Oscars stage in 1969. Streisand was accepting the best actress award, which she shared with Katharine Hepburn (Harvey was accepting on Hepburn’s behalf).

item.title: ‘The Singers’ and ‘Two People Exchanging Saliva’ Tie for Oscar
item.description: “I’m not joking,” Kumail Nanjiani said while presenting the award for best live action short film.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Towfighi

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kumail Nanjiani announces one of the two winners for best live action short.

item.title: Barbra Streisand Pays Tribute to Robert Redford at 2026 Oscars
item.description: Streisand and Redford built a lengthy friendship after acting together in 1973’s “The Way We Were.”
item.dc:creator: Michaela Towfighi

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “He was thoughtful and bold,” Barbra Streisand said of Robert Redford. “I called him an intellectual cowboy who blazed his own trail.”

item.title: Billy Crystal Leads 2026 Oscars Tribute to Rob and Michele Reiner
item.description: Billy Crystal led a tribute to the director several months after he and his wife were murdered.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Towfighi

media:content.media:credit: Frederic J. Brown/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Rob Reiner and his wife, Michele Singer Reiner, were killed in December.

item.title: Un veterano militar de EE. UU. acusado de un golpe de Estado fallido en Venezuela está prófugo
item.description: El gobierno estadounidense dijo que el ex boina verde, Jordan Goudreau, llevaba meses desaparecido y que se había encontrado un monitor de tobillo que se le había asignado escondido en un mueble.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich and Santul Nerkar
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
United States Army Special Forces (Green Berets)

media:content.media:credit: Chris O'Meara/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: El ex boina verde Jordan Goudreau mientras se dirigía al Tribunal Federal de EE. UU. para una audiencia de fianza en Tampa, Florida, el año pasado.

item.title: Anna Wintour and Anne Hathaway Riff on ‘The Devil Wears Prada’ at the Oscars
item.description: The Vogue empress presented alongside Anne Hathaway on Sunday evening for a not-so-subtle riff on “The Devil Wears Prada.”
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Borrowing a joke from the beloved fashion world sendup, Anna Wintour called Anne Hathaway “Emily" at one point as they presented the awards for best costume design and best makeup and hairstyling.

item.title: Conan O’Brien Parodies ‘Weapons’ and Roasts Timothée Chalamet in 2026 Oscars Monologue
item.description: In his second turn as Oscars host, his monologue wasn’t as loopy as last year’s, but he displayed a range of jokes — nerdy, topical and physical.
item.dc:creator: Jason Zinoman

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Conan O’Brien, dressed as Gladys in “Weapons,” led children on a chase through several movie scenes.

item.title: Sean Penn Wins Best Supporting Actor for ‘One Battle After Another’ but Skips Oscars
item.description: The actor has a history with the country’s president, Volodymyr Zelensky, and had been skipping awards ceremonies.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Bernstein

media:content.media:credit: Arnaud Finistre/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Sean Penn at a French festival in October.

item.title: Petróleo y estrecho de Ormuz: lo que hay que saber
item.description: El estrecho sigue siendo la única vía para exportar la mayor parte de la energía que se produce en la región. La geografía y los conflictos regionales complican su operación.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca F. Elliott and Vivian Nereim
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Sahar Al Attar/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Un buque de guerra navegando este mes en el estrecho de Ormuz. La vía fluvial sigue siendo la única opción para exportar la mayor parte de la energía producida en la región.

item.title: Vote for Your Favorite (and Least Favorite) Red Carpet Looks from the Oscars
item.description: You’ve seen the fashion from the Oscars red carpet. Now, the Styles desk wants you to rank your favorites.

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: In Illinois, Clashes Over AIPAC Erupt As House Campaign Closes
item.description: In a Democratic primary, accusations are flying that allies of a hard-line pro-Israel group are trying to divide progressives, exploiting a broader rift in the party.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Nam Y. Huh/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Bushra Amiwala, a Democratic candidate for Congress, in Chicago last month.

item.title: No Corrections: March 16, 2026
item.description: No corrections appeared in print on Monday, March 16, 2026.

item.title: Amy Madigan Wins Oscar for Best Supporting Actress in ‘Weapons’
item.description: The actress won for her role as Aunt Gladys in Zach Cregger’s horror hit.
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Morgan

media:content.media:credit: Philip Cheung for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Amy Madigan accepting the supporting actress Oscar for her role in “Weapons.”

item.title: Oscar 2026 en vivo: esta es la lista de ganadores
item.description: Los actores, directores, películas y otros ganadores de la 98.ª edición de los Premios de la Academia.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Gonzalez

media:content.media:credit: Sinna Nasseri for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The scene at the Academy Awards.

item.title: Confidential Report Calls for Sweeping Changes to Track Covid Vaccine Harms
item.description: A federal work group says Covid vaccine injuries deserve urgent attention, even as Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. shifts away from talking about vaccine policy.
item.dc:creator: Apoorva Mandavilli and Sheryl Gay Stolberg
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

media:content.media:credit: Alyssa Pointer/Reuters
media:content.media:description: The members of the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices meeting at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention in Atlanta in December.

item.title: Our Favorite Oscars Red Carpet Photos Featuring Jessie Buckley and Emma Stone
item.description: Grand entrances, statement-making fashion and the occasional candid moment, on a night geared for gold.
item.dc:creator: Sinna Nasseri

media:content.media:credit: Sinna Nasseri for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Teyana Taylor, nominated for best supporting actress in “One Battle After Another.”

item.title: Highlights From the 2026 Oscars: ‘One Battle After Another’ Wins Six Awards, Including Best Picture
item.description: Paul Thomas Anderson’s film also took best director and best supporting actor for Sean Penn. Michael B. Jordan won best actor for “Sinners,” and Jessie Buckley was named best actress for “Hamnet.”
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Jutharat Pinyodoonyachet for The New York Times

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
